title: Deep Learning Approaches to Climate Prediction
abstract: We present a convolutional neural network architecture for medium-range weather forecasting trained on ERA5 reanalysis data. The model outperforms numerical weather prediction baselines on 5-day precipitation forecasts.

title: Fairness Constraints in Algorithmic Decision Making: A Legal Perspective
abstract: This paper analyses the tension between statistical fairness criteria and EU non-discrimination law, arguing that disparate impact doctrine imposes constraints that are incompatible with demographic parity requirements.

title: Large Language Models for Clinical Note Summarisation
abstract: We fine-tune a 7B parameter LLM on de-identified clinical notes and evaluate summarisation quality using ROUGE and physician ratings. Our model reduces documentation time by 34% in a pilot study at a university hospital.

title: Causal Inference in Observational Health Studies Using Propensity Score Matching
abstract: We compare propensity score matching, inverse probability weighting, and doubly robust estimation in a cohort study of antihypertensive medication adherence and cardiovascular outcomes.

title: Urban Heat Islands and Social Vulnerability: A Spatial Analysis
abstract: Using satellite land surface temperature data and census indicators we show that urban heat island intensity correlates with neighbourhood-level social vulnerability scores across 40 European cities.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Portfolio Optimisation Under Transaction Costs
abstract: We formulate dynamic portfolio selection as a Markov decision process and train a proximal policy optimisation agent on historical equity returns. The agent learns to balance turnover penalties against expected return.

title: Ontology Alignment for Cross-Institutional Research Data Sharing
abstract: We introduce a transformer-based ontology alignment system that maps heterogeneous institutional metadata schemas to a common upper ontology, enabling federated search across four national research information systems.

title: Multilingual Sentiment Analysis of Parliamentary Debates
abstract: We apply cross-lingual transfer learning to classify sentiment in parliamentary speeches from 12 EU member states. A single model fine-tuned on English debates achieves 83% accuracy on zero-shot transfer to Dutch, German, and French corpora.

title: Graph Neural Networks for Drug-Target Interaction Prediction
abstract: We represent molecular structures as attributed graphs and train a message-passing neural network to predict binding affinity. Our model achieves state-of-the-art results on the Davis and KIBA benchmarks without hand-crafted molecular descriptors.

title: Participatory Budgeting and Local Democracy: Evidence from a Randomised Controlled Trial
abstract: We randomly assign 60 Portuguese municipalities to a participatory budgeting programme and track civic engagement, budget allocation, and subjective wellbeing over three years. Treatment municipalities show a 12% increase in voter turnout at local elections.

title: Federated Learning with Differential Privacy for Healthcare Data
abstract: We propose a federated training protocol that combines secure aggregation with Rényi differential privacy guarantees, enabling hospitals to jointly train a mortality prediction model without sharing raw patient records.

title: The Gender Pay Gap in Academia: A Longitudinal Analysis
abstract: Using administrative salary data from 28 European universities over 15 years, we decompose the gender pay gap into explained and unexplained components using Blinder–Oaxaca decomposition. The unexplained gap persists at 8–11% after controlling for rank, discipline, and tenure.
